title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, from South Boston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team BMC to support hospital social workers.
The fundraising goal is set at $10,000 to aid Boston Medical Center's mission of providing care regardless of patients' financial status.
Beattie aims to raise awareness for the essential work of social workers who support patients in crisis at the hospital.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave, extending their search for a first NWSL win.
Coach Filipa Patão highlighted the team's need for patience in the final third during attacks.
Amanda Gutierres struggled with passing accuracy, registering 11 of 21 completed passes against San Diego.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr., a suspended attorney from Melrose, faces charges of stealing hundreds of thousands from clients.
Rappa was arraigned on embezzlement, larceny, and unauthorized practice of law in Malden District Court.
He allegedly continued practicing law after suspension, mishandling client funds despite court orders to desist.

title: Eugene Mirman posts message after fiery NH crash and dramatic rescue
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eugene Mirman, "Bob's Burgers" actor, was rescued from a burning vehicle after crashing near Bedford Toll Plaza, NH.
Mirman thanked rescuers and hospital staff in a social media post after being treated for serious injuries from the crash.
The crash remains under investigation, and New Hampshire police are seeking information from the public.

title: NH trooper assaulted in unprovoked attack
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Jonathan G. Newcomb, 53, allegedly attacked a New Hampshire State Trooper in Dover while holding a stick and metal tool.
Newcomb faces felony and misdemeanor charges including criminal threatening, firearm theft attempt, and assault on a police officer.
Both the trooper and Newcomb were treated for minor injuries at Wentworth-Douglass Hospital in Dover and later released.

title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett, 24, is running the Boston Marathon again to support Mass General after a personal loss.
Mallett honors Sharla Sieve, who died from metastatic colorectal cancer, and raises funds for better cancer treatment.
He struggled but finished the marathon last year and aims to complete it again in her memory.

title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives anticipate the Patriots trading for Eagles WR A.J. Brown after June 1 for salary cap reasons.
A potential trade for A.J. Brown could include a first-round pick; Philadelphia seeks significant compensation.
Patriots maintain interest in acquiring WR talent despite recent signing of WR Romeo Doubs to a four-year deal.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Testament of Ann Lee' featuring Amanda Seyfried is streaming on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' directed by Nia DaCosta is available on Netflix.
'Ken Burns Presents: Henry David Thoreau' documentary is streaming on PBS.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox defeat Padres 5-2 in home opener at Fenway Park, ending a five-game losing streak.
Sonny Gray pitched six innings, allowing two runs, contributing to Boston's victory.
Willson Contreras hit his first home run with the Red Sox, showcasing his influence on the team.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots' offseason workouts start with phase one on April 20, focusing on conditioning and meetings.
Phase two in May features on-field drills without contact for the New England Patriots.
Patriots' OTAs are scheduled for May 27, May 29, June 1-2, June 4, June 8-9, and June 11, followed by a mandatory minicamp June 15-17.
